title: Free Play-to-Earn Games With No Investment (2026): Including a Crypto MMA Battle Royale
html:
Play to earn games without investment do exist in 2026, but the honest list is far shorter than the marketing suggests. A game is only genuinely no-deposit when you can install it, play it and reach a real earning mechanic without ever buying a token, an NFT or a paid "starter pack". By that strict test, most titles that advertise themselves as free quietly fail. This guide names the free P2E games that pass, flags the ones that hide an NFT gate behind the word "free", and shows you how to start earning without spending a penny.

We have kept the earnings talk deliberately grounded. Nobody can promise you an income from a game, and anyone who does is selling something. What we can do is separate the genuinely free-to-start entries from the pay-to-win economies wearing a free costume.

What "free play to earn games" actually means

The phrase gets stretched to breaking point. Three very different things all get sold as "free":

  • Truly free to start. You download or open the game, play, and can earn without ever paying. Fees, if any, are tiny network gas costs rather than a purchase. This is what most people mean by play to earn no deposit.
  • Free to install, paid to earn. The download is free, but rewards are locked behind a one-off "spellbook", "starter deck" or activation NFT. You can look around, but you cannot actually earn until you spend.
  • Free demo, pay to win. Free access exists, but the meaningful economy runs on NFTs you are strongly nudged to buy. Skill barely moves the needle against a funded wallet.

Only the first category honestly answers the search for play to earn games without investment. The rest are worth knowing about, but you should walk in with your eyes open. When a listicle ranks twenty "free" games without drawing this line, treat the whole thing as an affiliate funnel.

If you want the wider framing of how earning-while-playing sits alongside watch-to-earn and move-to-earn, our explainer on DeHub watch-to-earn versus play-to-earn breaks down where each model actually pays.

The best free P2E games in 2026 that pass the no-investment test

Below are titles you can genuinely start for free. We lead with DeHub's own arcade because it is built around no upfront cost by design, then cover the reputable outside options and exactly where each one draws its paywall.

Last Chad Standing (DeHub arcade)

Last Chad Standing is DeHub's flagship game: an MMA fighter battle royale featuring licensed fighters, playable straight from the browser at the DeHub app and through the arcade in the Google Play build. There is no NFT to buy before you fight and no starter pack to unlock the mode. You open it and play.

What makes it a clean fit for this list is the plumbing around it. DeHub is a decentralised social network where the arcade sits next to the feed, live streaming and watch-to-earn bounties, so the game is one earning surface among several rather than a walled-off token machine. Rewards flow through the ecosystem token, $DHB, on Base. We will not quote you a figure, because your results depend on how you play and on live conditions, and honest coverage does not invent numbers.

The practical appeal is the zero-friction start. Because DeHub's arcade is play-to-earn with no deposit, you can try the battle royale, see whether the loop clicks and only then decide how deep to go. There is a full breakdown in our guide to Last Chad Standing, the crypto MMA battle royale.

The wider DeHub arcade and watch-to-earn

Beyond the flagship title, DeHub runs a play-to-earn arcade plus watch-to-earn bounties that reward attention rather than purchases. This matters for the no-investment crowd: even if a given game does not suit you, the same free account lets you earn by watching and engaging. It is a more forgiving on-ramp than a single game that only pays skilled competitive players. You can open all of it from the DeHub app or the DeHub Android app on Google Play.

Gods Unchained

Gods Unchained is a skill-based trading card game and one of the genuinely free-to-start options outside the DeHub ecosystem in 2026. You can install it on PC or Mac, pick up starter decks and compete in ranked play without buying cards first. Rewards come as GODS tokens and card NFTs earned through matches, and because outcomes hinge on deck-building and play skill rather than wallet size, it is one of the fairer entries for someone starting from zero. Expect a learning curve: the earning ranks reward players who understand the meta, not casual dabblers.

Illuvium

Illuvium offers a free-to-play route into an open-world creature-collector with auto-battler combat. You can explore and play the core loop without an upfront NFT purchase, which puts it on the honest side of the line. Be realistic about hardware: it is a graphically heavy game, so it leans towards desktop players rather than the mobile play to earn games audience.

Pixels

Pixels is a browser-based farming and social game with free starter options that let you begin playing, and earning through in-game activity, without paying in. As with any free tier, the most lucrative land and upgrades sit behind paid assets, so treat the free track as the way to learn the loop rather than a guaranteed income. It is one of the more accessible best free P2E games for players who prefer a laid-back, mobile-friendly pace over competitive combat.

Free games that hide an NFT gate (read before you start)

These come up constantly on "no investment" lists, and the label is only half true. Knowing where the gate sits saves you a wasted evening.

Splinterlands

Splinterlands is frequently listed as free, and you can indeed create an account and look around for nothing. The catch is the Summoner's Spellbook: on most accounts, unlocking the ability to actually earn rewards requires a one-off purchase of roughly ten US dollars' worth of credits. That is a small sum, but it is still a deposit, which disqualifies it from a strict play to earn no deposit list. It is a perfectly reasonable game; just do not believe the "completely free" framing.

"Free mint" and "free starter NFT" games

Be wary of any game whose free tier is a free NFT mint. The mint may be free, but you often pay network gas to claim it, and the earning economy is usually tuned so that the free asset earns a trickle while paid assets earn the real rewards. This is the free-to-win-nothing pattern. If a game's own documentation cannot show you an earning path that needs zero spend, assume there isn't one.

Anything demanding a token deposit "to activate rewards"

A recurring scam shape is the game that lets you accrue "earnings" on a dashboard, then asks for a deposit, an "activation fee" or a "gas top-up sent to this address" before you can withdraw. Legitimate games never require you to send tokens to an external address to unlock your own winnings. Treat that request as an exit signal.

How to start earning without spending anything

You can get moving in an afternoon without opening your wallet for a purchase.

  1. Pick a genuinely free-to-start title. From the list above, that means DeHub's arcade and Last Chad Standing, Gods Unchained, Illuvium or Pixels' free track.
  2. Set up a self-custody wallet. A standard Ethereum-compatible wallet works for Base, where DeHub's $DHB lives, as well as most major P2E chains. Creating a wallet is free.
  3. Play the free loop first. Learn the mechanics and confirm you actually enjoy it before considering any optional spend. Enjoyment is the only thing that makes the hours worthwhile when rewards are modest.
  4. Stack complementary earning surfaces. On DeHub, the same free account gives you the arcade, watch-to-earn bounties and a chronological social feed, so your time compounds across several mechanics rather than one.
  5. Keep records from day one. Even small crypto rewards can be taxable. If you are in the UK, read our guide to play-to-earn earnings and HMRC tax in the UK before your first payout so nothing surprises you later.

How to spot a P2E scam (and find games that actually pay)

The uncomfortable truth is that "play to earn games that actually pay" is a small subset of everything marketed as P2E. Use these filters:

  • The deposit test. If earning requires an upfront purchase, it is not no-investment, whatever the homepage says. That is not automatically a scam, but it changes the maths.
  • The withdrawal test. Can real players withdraw real value today? Search for recent, dated reports of successful cash-outs, not launch-day hype.
  • The transparency test. Is the token real and traceable on a public chain and listed on aggregators like CoinGecko or CoinMarketCap? DeHub's $DHB is on Base with contracts also on BNB Chain and Polygon, listed on both aggregators and tradable on Coinbase, which is the kind of verifiability you want.
  • The pressure test. Urgency, guaranteed returns, "send tokens to activate", and referral-only earning are all red flags. No honest game promises income.
  • The team test. Named, accountable people beat anonymous founders. DeHub is built by DeLabs Ltd, a United Kingdom company, co-founded by Mike Hales and Indi Jay Cammish.

For a deeper account of what separates a working model from a Ponzi in disguise, our wider DeHub decentralised social network hub connects the arcade to the rest of the ecosystem, so you can see the earning is not the whole product.

Mobile play to earn games: what to expect in 2026

If you want to play on a phone, your realistic options narrow. Graphically heavy titles like Illuvium favour desktop, while card and social games travel better to mobile. DeHub is built mobile-first: the Android app on Google Play carries the arcade, Last Chad Standing, watch-to-earn and the social feed in one install, which is about as close to a one-tap no-deposit start as the category offers in 2026. Whatever you choose, budget for battery drain and for the fact that the smoothest mobile experiences still tend to be the free-to-start ones rather than the NFT-heavy economies.

Frequently asked questions

Are there really play to earn games without investment?

Yes, but fewer than the marketing implies. Genuinely no-deposit options in 2026 include DeHub's arcade and Last Chad Standing, Gods Unchained, Illuvium and Pixels' free track. The key is confirming you can reach an actual earning mechanic without buying a token, NFT or starter pack. Many "free" games only let you play for free while gating rewards behind a purchase.

Which free P2E games actually pay?

The ones worth your time have a real, traceable token, a public earning path and dated evidence of players cashing out. Skill-based titles like Gods Unchained reward understanding over spending, and DeHub ties its arcade to watch-to-earn and a live social ecosystem with $DHB on Base. Be sceptical of any game that cannot show you an earning route requiring zero upfront spend.

Is Splinterlands free to play?

You can create an account and browse for free, but earning rewards on most accounts requires the Summoner's Spellbook, a one-off purchase of around ten US dollars' worth of credits. That makes it free to install but not strictly no-deposit. It is a legitimate game, so just do not rely on the "completely free" framing when you are budgeting your time.

What is the catch with free-to-start crypto games?

Usually one of three things: rewards are modest for the hours involved, the best assets sit behind optional paid NFTs, or the "free" tier is a funnel towards spending. None of that makes a game a scam, but it does mean you should play for enjoyment first and treat any earnings as a bonus. Genuine scams reveal themselves by demanding a deposit to "unlock" or "withdraw" your own winnings.

Do I have to pay tax on play-to-earn rewards in the UK?

Often, yes. Crypto received from playing can be treated as income when you receive it, and disposing of it later can trigger capital gains, depending on your circumstances. HMRC guidance evolves, so keep dated records of every reward from the start and read our play-to-earn UK and HMRC tax guide before your first meaningful payout.

Where can I start for free right now?

Open the DeHub app in a browser to try Last Chad Standing and the arcade with no deposit, or install the DeHub app on Google Play for the mobile-first experience with the arcade, watch-to-earn and the social feed in one place.
